channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: Times Music Buys Punjabi Music Catalog to Boost India Music Holdings
item.description: Times Music, partnered with Primary Wave Music, has acquired Catrack Entertainment, securing a significant 1990s Punjabi music catalog. This marks Times Music's third such purchase, reinforcing its strategy to build music rights in India's diverse market. The company aims to profit from this legacy content through increased streaming, music licensing for media, and brand deals, capitalizing on growing global demand for Indian music. The move signals Times Music's strong focus on regional music rights, positioning it as a key buyer in the sector.

item.title: Classic Legends Hits Profit; EV, Competition Challenge Growth
item.description: Classic Legends achieved profitability in FY26, boosting sales by 40% to 45,409 units. Managing Director Anupam Thareja forecasts stronger FY27 profits from four new motorcycle launches and expanding exports. However, the company maintains a cautious EV approach due to profitability concerns, unlike competitors like Royal Enfield and Triumph.

item.title: Thomas Cook India Stock Tumbles Despite Forex Card Expansion
item.description: Thomas Cook India has expanded its prepaid forex card to 28 currencies, adding 16 new denominations to capture shifting travel patterns towards emerging destinations. This allows direct spending in local currencies, avoiding costly conversion layers. Despite the innovation, the stock slipped 1.25% on Thursday. The company's shares are down over 30% year-long, suggesting investor skepticism about its financial health despite new product offerings.

item.title: Indian Banks Scrutinize Anthropic's Mythos AI for Software Flaws
item.description: India's banks are being gathered by the Indian Banks' Association (IBA) to evaluate threats from Anthropic's Mythos AI, specifically its power to find software bugs. This effort follows a key meeting emphasizing the need to toughen IT defenses against rising cyber risks.

item.title: JPMorgan Rates Meesho 'Overweight' But Sees Margin Hurdles Ahead
item.description: JPMorgan has initiated coverage on Meesho with an 'Overweight' rating and a ₹215 price target, forecasting significant growth in Net Merchandise Value (NMV) and improved EBITDA margins. While the brokerage predicts a 23% NMV CAGR and 4% EBITDA margins by FY31, Meesho currently trades with a negative P/E ratio, indicating profitability struggles. The target price suggests substantial upside, but competition and difficulties in scaling ad revenue pose execution risks.

item.title: IIFL Finance Climbs on Strong Q4 Beat, Despite Ongoing Stock Slump
item.description: IIFL Finance shares climbed nearly 6% on April 30, 2026, following robust Q4 FY26 earnings that showed a 148% year-on-year profit increase and 38% AUM growth. However, the stock's 27% year-to-date decline against a flattish Nifty50 index highlights ongoing investor scrutiny despite the positive quarterly performance.

item.title: EPFO Plans ₹7,500 Pension Hike, ATM Withdrawals for Members
item.description: The Employees' Provident Fund Organisation (EPFO) is planning major reforms. These include potentially raising the minimum monthly pension to ₹7,500, introducing ATM-based fund withdrawals, and offering an 8.25% interest credit for FY 2023-24. These steps aim to improve subscriber welfare and access. EPFO also reported processing over 8.31 crore claims in FY 2025-26, with automation speeding up settlements.

item.title: Bajaj Finance: 22% Profit Jump, 24% AUM Growth Goal Amid Premium Valuation
item.description: Bajaj Finance posted strong Q4 FY26 results, with profit rising 22% year-on-year and guidance for 22-24% Asset Under Management (AUM) growth in FY27. Despite operational strengths and expansion, the NBFC's premium valuation and sector headwinds like rising costs and regulatory scrutiny prompt a neutral outlook, with limited near-term catalysts for a stock upgrade.

item.title: IIFL Finance Posts 148% Q4 Profit Jump; Valuation Worries Persist
item.description: IIFL Finance delivered strong Q4 FY26 earnings, driven by robust gold loan growth and improved efficiency, resulting in a 148% year-on-year profit increase. Motilal Oswal reiterated its BUY rating with a ₹600 price target. However, persistent worries about high valuations, concentration risk in gold loans, and past regulatory scrutiny temper optimism, especially compared to diversified peers like Bajaj Finance.

item.title: Park Medi World Stock Gains on Analyst 'BUY' Amid Valuation Doubts
item.description: Choice Institutional Equities initiated coverage on Park Medi World (PARKHOSP) with a 'BUY' rating and a INR 320 target price. The firm highlights the hospital operator's capital-efficient, self-funded expansion plan to grow from 3,960 beds to over 10,000 in five years, including acquiring distressed assets. However, its current market valuation appears high compared to peers, raising questions about its growth sustainability and risks.

item.title: Viasat Completes Global Satellite Network with APAC Launch
item.description: Viasat Inc. has successfully launched its final ViaSat-3 satellite, completing its advanced global constellation and extending its services to the Asia-Pacific region. This new satellite offers over 1 Terabit per second of capacity, allowing Viasat to direct bandwidth to areas with high demand. The satellite is set to begin service in late 2026, strengthening Viasat's position in the competitive global connectivity market.

item.title: Motilal Oswal Tips Federal Bank, IIFL, Indian Bank, Five Star for Growth
item.description: Motilal Oswal has identified four financial stocks poised for growth, assigning 'Buy' ratings based on earnings and valuation. Federal Bank and Indian Bank show improved asset quality and deposit strategies. IIFL Finance benefits from gold loan momentum, while Five Star Business Finance signals a recovery. These picks come amid evolving regulations and sector pressures.

item.title: RBL Bank: SEBI OKs Emirates NBD Deal, But Valuation Fears Drag Stock
item.description: RBL Bank shares dipped slightly even after getting key approval from India's SEBI for Emirates NBD's investment. The deal, which gives the foreign lender significant control, still has conditions. The market's cool response points to worries about RBL Bank's high valuation compared to rivals, despite strong foreign investment in India's financial sector. Analysts are cautiously optimistic, seeing little immediate stock gain.

item.title: India's Proactive Tax Planning Fuels Wealth Management Boom
item.description: Indian taxpayers are increasingly recognizing the benefits of early tax planning, moving beyond last-minute investments. This proactive approach fuels demand for structured financial products like ELSS funds and NPS, driving growth in the wealth management and advisory sectors. Financial institutions are adapting with personalized services and digital platforms to capture this expanding market, while regulatory changes and a robust economic outlook further shape investment behavior.

item.title: Adani's Cemindia Soars 20% on Huge Profit Jump, Margin Gains, New Orders
item.description: Cemindia Projects shares jumped sharply, hitting a 20% upper circuit, after reporting robust Q4 FY26 results. The company posted an 113.63% year-on-year net profit increase to ₹242.17 crore. Revenue grew 17.4% to ₹2,973.49 crore, boosted by an improved EBITDA margin that widened to 15.1% from 10.7%. Cemindia also secured ₹14,821 crore in new contracts during FY26, growing its order book to ₹24,545 crore, benefiting from its integration with the Adani Group.

item.title: India's Small & Midcaps Post 12-Year April Rally; Geopolitics Looms
item.description: Indian small and midcap stocks are celebrating their biggest monthly gain in 12 years this April. The BSE Smallcap index jumped 20.1% and the BSE Midcap index rose 14.8%. The rally was fueled by positive Q4 earnings expectations and strong retail investor interest, making valuations appealing after a slow 2025. However, experts warn of upcoming market choppiness, stressing the importance of careful stock picking due to potential volatility.

item.title: MOIL Profit Falls 20% as Aging Mines Hike Costs; Stock Dips 5.5%
item.description: MOIL's net profit fell 20% year-on-year to ₹92.6 crore in Q4 FY26, driven by total expenses rising 6.2% to ₹353.53 crore. Despite a 2.5% revenue increase to ₹433.39 crore, higher costs caused MOIL's share price to drop 5.5% to ₹314.9 on April 30, 2026, lagging the wider market. The results raise concerns about how aging mining infrastructure impacts the company's profitability.

item.title: RBI Opens Early Redemption for Gold Bonds with 295% Returns
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India is allowing early redemption for Sovereign Gold Bonds 2019-20 Series VI starting April 30. Investors stand to gain nearly 295 percent, with a ₹1 lakh investment now worth approximately ₹3.95 lakh. However, new tax rules implemented from April 1 mean capital gains on premature redemption are now taxable, even for original subscribers.

item.title: Federal Bank exits Deutsche India deal, Kotak Mahindra sole bidder
item.description: Federal Bank has withdrawn from acquisition talks for Deutsche Bank's Indian retail and wealth management operations, positioning Kotak Mahindra Bank as the sole potential buyer for the estimated ₹4,500 crore deal. This move signals Federal Bank's strategic focus on organic expansion of its wealth management business, leveraging its 'FedNext' platform. The development occurs as foreign banks reassess their presence in India due to intense domestic competition and regulatory pressures, while Kotak Mahindra Bank stands to significantly deepen its retail footprint.

item.title: 5% Treasury Yield Triggers Risk-Off Selloff for Bitcoin, Stocks
item.description: The 30-year US Treasury yield has surged past 5%, a level not consistently seen since 2007, raising inflation concerns driven by higher oil prices and a hawkish Federal Reserve. This is tightening financial conditions, making safer assets more attractive and pressuring Bitcoin, which fell amid a stronger dollar. Dissent at the Fed suggests interest rates may stay higher for longer, impacting tech stocks and gold.

item.title: Emkay Global Raises Star Health Target to Rs 650 on BUY Call
item.description: Star Health reported strong Q4 results, driven by a significantly improved claims ratio to 64.8%. Emkay Global Financial reiterates its 'BUY' rating, raising the target price to Rs 650. The firm cites consistent improvement in claims ratio and a focus on profitable growth, projecting high-teen premium growth and mid-to-high-teen RoEs.

item.title: CEAT's Q4 Triumph Fades as Raw Material Costs Surge
item.description: CEAT reported a strong Q4 FY26 with a 145% net profit jump and record revenue. However, the company faces a challenging outlook due to an expected 15-20% hike in raw material costs for Q1 FY27. Management's planned price increases could temper demand and squeeze margins, leading Emkay Global to downgrade CEAT to 'REDUCE' with a lower price target.

item.title: HUL Profit Jumps 18% But Stock Falls on Geopolitical Volatility
item.description: Hindustan Unilever's Q4 FY26 profit rose 18% to ₹29.30 billion, boosted by tax savings and strong home-care sales. Despite this, shares dropped nearly 3% due to market concerns over commodity and currency swings caused by Middle East conflict. The company is raising prices and cutting costs to manage these impacts but is holding its medium-term margin outlook. Competitors like Nestle India saw stock gains from solid volume growth and stable margins.

item.title: India Stocks Surge in April on Short Covering, Not Real Strength
item.description: India's stock market ended the April derivatives series with significant gains, including a 7.45% rise for the Nifty 50 and a 10.19% surge for the Bank Nifty. However, derivative data shows this rally was mainly driven by short covering, not new buyer conviction. Open interest dropped sharply, and rollover participation remained low, indicating traders closed existing positions instead of opening new ones. Sectoral performance varied, with Pharma and Oil & Gas showing interest, while IT stocks saw renewed bearish sentiment.

item.title: Cognizant's 'Project Leap' AI Overhaul Aims for $300M Savings, Lifts Margin Guidance
item.description: Cognizant is launching 'Project Leap,' a major restructuring effort to integrate AI and save costs. The program aims for $200 million to $300 million in annual savings starting in 2026, fully realized by 2027. Funds will reinvest in AI capabilities and workforce training. Cognizant also raised its 2026 adjusted operating margin guidance to 16%-16.2%. The initiative involves an estimated $230 million to $320 million in costs, mainly for employee severance.

item.title: Cemindia Projects Stock Soars to ₹808.40 After Strong FY26 Results
item.description: Cemindia Projects Ltd. saw its stock jump to ₹808.40 on Thursday, April 30, 2026. This surge followed strong Q4 and full-year FY26 financial results where profits grew much faster than revenue. The company plans to reinvest earnings for future projects instead of paying a dividend, even as the broader market weakened.

item.title: IIFL Finance Profit Jumps 148% in Q4 FY26 on Gold Loans, Strategic Exits
item.description: IIFL Finance delivered a robust Q4 FY26, marked by a 148% year-on-year surge in net profit to ₹623 crore, fueled by exceptional growth in its gold loan portfolio which saw AUM jump 150% YoY to ₹52,581 crore. This performance is underpinned by a strategic pivot away from riskier lending avenues, leading to significant improvements in asset quality with Gross NPAs falling to 1.5%. The company's total income rose 51% to ₹2,090 crore, signaling a strong recovery and reinforcing a positive outlook, supported by broad analyst consensus for growth.

item.title: India Stocks Plunge on Oil Price Surge; AI Valued High, Sun Pharma Buys
item.description: Indian stocks plunged Thursday, with the Sensex down over 1,100 points, as oil prices jumped above $125 and the rupee hit a record low. This market slump contrasted with the booming AI sector, where Anthropic PBC is exploring funding at over $900 billion. Other news includes Sun Pharma's $11.75 billion acquisition of Organon, Renault India's restructuring plans, record ETF inflows in FY26, and upcoming RBI rules affecting bank provisioning.

item.title: Bajaj Auto Jumps on Buyback News; Valuation Test Looms
item.description: Bajaj Auto's stock surged on news of a potential share buyback, outperforming a declining market. The company's board will consider the buyback, alongside fiscal year-end financial results and dividend recommendations, on May 6. While the buyback offers a short-term boost, analysts and investors will scrutinize the upcoming financial disclosures to justify the company's premium valuation compared to peers.

item.title: Oil Surges Past $126 on Mideast Conflict; Bitcoin Tested
item.description: Middle East tensions have sent Brent crude oil prices soaring past $126 a barrel, adding a geopolitical risk premium to energy costs. This surge is testing Bitcoin (BTC) and other cryptocurrencies, which are seeing price drops alongside traditional risk assets. The situation occurs against a backdrop of concerns about inflation and central bank interest rate policies.

item.title: Bajaj Finance Posts Strong Results, Analysts Question Valuation
item.description: Bajaj Finance reported strong Q4 earnings of ₹54.6 billion PAT, driven by lower credit costs, and offered an optimistic FY27 outlook with 22-24% AUM growth. However, Emkay Global kept its 'REDUCE' rating and raised the target to ₹950, citing persistent valuation concerns with its FY28 implied P/B multiple still high compared to peers.

item.title: SIFs Race to ₹10,000 Crore; Investors Urged to Proceed with Caution
item.description: Specialised Investment Funds (SIFs) have quickly gathered over ₹10,000 crore in assets since launching six months ago. While sophisticated strategies and new products from asset managers are driving interest, complex execution, higher costs, and a shortage of trained distributors mean investors need to carefully check if these advanced products are right for them.

item.title: Meta's AI Spending Spree Sinks Stock; Alphabet, Amazon Surge on Earnings
item.description: Big Tech giants Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, and Microsoft reported Q1 earnings amid a massive AI infrastructure spending race. The four hyperscalers plan to spend a combined $650 billion on AI by 2026. While all beat revenue forecasts, market reactions diverged sharply. Meta Platforms saw its stock tumble due to increased spending guidance without a clear monetization plan. Alphabet and Amazon shares surged on strong cloud growth and earnings beats. Microsoft's stock remained flat despite strong results, weighed by free cash flow concerns.

item.title: India Stocks Plunge Over 1.5% as Oil Surges Past $120, Rupee Hits Record Low
item.description: Indian equity benchmarks Sensex and Nifty plunged over 1.5% Thursday, with the Sensex dropping over 1,100 points. Key triggers included Brent crude oil breaching $120, pushing inflation fears, and the Indian rupee hitting an all-time low. Foreign investors continued large outflows, while geopolitical tensions and a cautious Fed stance fueled market volatility.

item.title: Torrent Power Raises ₹4,000 Cr Via Bonds for Coal Plant Buyout
item.description: Torrent Power is raising ₹4,000 crore through its largest-ever corporate bond sale to buy Nabha Power's coal plant. This acquisition boosts the company's thermal capacity, a move that contrasts with the shift to renewables and raises questions about valuation and debt in India's power sector. The bond sale also signals a rise in companies using debt markets for big acquisitions.

item.title: HUL Q4: Profit Jumps 21% Amid Rising Costs and Volatility
item.description: Hindustan Unilever (HUL) reported a 21% rise in Q4 net profit to ₹2,994 crore on 8% revenue growth to ₹16,207 crore and 6% volume increase. However, profit excluding exceptional items grew a slower 4% to ₹2,711 crore, showing margin pressure from global volatility and costs. HUL proposed a ₹22 final dividend. Its P/E of 46.19 is just below the FMCG sector average amid ongoing market uncertainty.

item.title: Jana SFB Q4 Profit Beat, Loan Growth Strong; Deposit Drop, License Hopes in Focus
item.description: Jana Small Finance Bank posted a 17% profit increase for Q4, beating expectations, thanks to strong Assets Under Management (AUM) growth of 23% and margin expansion. Systematix maintained its 'Buy' rating with a ₹520 target. However, a drop in customer deposits to 17.9% and past execution concerns create challenges, even as the bank seeks a universal banking license, a prospect not yet factored into current estimates.

item.title: Vedanta Stock Dips After Demerger as Market Revalues Shares
item.description: Vedanta shares fell sharply on April 30, 2026, after trading ex-demerger. Millions of shares changed hands as the market reacted to the split, despite strong Q4 results driven by higher commodity prices and operational gains. Brokerages are positive, but the immediate drop raises questions about how the market values the newly separate entities, especially the core Vedanta holding assets like Hindustan Zinc.

item.title: Indian Bank Shares Tumble as Provisions Surge on West Asia Crisis
item.description: Indian Bank shares dropped sharply by over 5.77% on April 30, 2026. This occurred despite Q4FY26 results showing a 5% net profit rise to ₹3,103 crore and 11.28% NII growth. The stock decline was mainly due to a 54.7% surge in provisions to ₹1,228 crore, including ₹3.1 billion for the West Asia crisis. Rising funding costs and a planned ₹5,000 crore capital raise also weighed on investor sentiment.
